mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls reflects aggregate income growth that drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows SPX 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS is increasing, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous 3-month decline in industrial production and a 0.2 percentage point rise in unemployment produce a joint recessionary signal that weakens earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances where both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or interaction term t-stat < 2.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a decline in retail sales signals deteriorating consumer demand that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of late-cycle months with declining RSAFS, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production month-over-month growth exceeding 0.3% indicates rising capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in fewer than 55% of triggered instances when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3% while the federal funds rate remains below that inflation rate, negative real rates reduce equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances with CPI > 3% and FEDFUNDS below CPI, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below their 6-month average signal tightening credit conditions and declining construction activity, which reduces aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances when HOUST falls below its 6-month average, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 2% annualized signals insufficient aggregate demand to sustain earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 2%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.
